title: Repo foundation
description: Establish the initial Roger Reviewer monorepo structure, operating docs, and architecture-spike track so later implementation is grounded in a coherent workspace rather than ad hoc package creation.
acceptance_criteria: - Workspace/package layout is defined\n- Core docs and planning artefacts are linked\n- Early architecture spikes and ADR work are captured as child tasks

title: Add an initial README and curate the first GitHub-facing planning snapshot
description: Create a contributor-facing top-level README for Roger Reviewer, clean up ignore rules for transient local artifacts, and define a lean first commit that captures the planning/docs core without vendoring heavyweight local exploration clones.

title: Domain and storage
description: Define the durable Roger data model for sessions, findings, artifacts, drafts, and search so the product has a stable source of truth before UI work spreads.
acceptance_criteria: - Core entities and invariants are explicit\n- Storage and migration approach is selected\n- Search baseline is included in the design

title: OpenCode session orchestration
description: Define and implement the Roger-to-OpenCode linkage so Roger can add structure without breaking plain OpenCode fallback and resume.
acceptance_criteria: - Session linkage boundary is explicit\n- Resume and compaction recovery expectations are testable\n- Roger metadata references underlying sessions rather than hiding them

title: Prompt pipeline and review engine
description: Build the staged review workflow that explores first, goes deep second, and persists structured findings rather than terminal-only output.
acceptance_criteria: - Prompt stages are explicit\n- Findings are structured and durable\n- Refresh flows can carry forward or resolve findings coherently

title: Session-aware CLI
description: Provide the shell entrypoint for starting, resuming, inspecting, and refreshing Roger review sessions using repo context where possible.
acceptance_criteria: - Review start/resume flow works from the shell\n- Status and findings inspection are available\n- CLI can drive the core review loop without the browser extension

title: TUI workflow
description: Implement the main Roger TUI for session navigation, findings triage, linked evidence review, and approval of outbound GitHub drafts.
acceptance_criteria: - Active review sessions are navigable in the TUI\n- Findings can be triaged quickly\n- Outbound drafts can be reviewed and approved locally

title: GitHub integration and extension
description: Add GitHub-targeted adapters and the browser entry surface without letting the extension become the system control plane or state owner.
acceptance_criteria: - GitHub adapter behavior is explicit\n- Browser launch bridge is validated\n- Extension UI provides launch/resume with bounded status expectations

title: Approval and posting flow
description: Model and implement the local draft and approval path for comments, questions, and suggestions before any GitHub mutation occurs.
acceptance_criteria: - Outbound drafts are explicit local objects\n- Approval is required before posting\n- Posted actions keep an audit trail and remote linkage

title: Worktrees and named instances
description: Support isolated review work via worktrees and conservative named-instance sharing without overengineering the first version.
acceptance_criteria: - Review environments can be isolated safely\n- Multiple named instances can coexist\n- State-sharing strategy is conservative and explicit

title: Search, memory, and skills
description: Add the retrieval layer for prior findings, searchable artifacts, and later semantic search or review-specific memory workflows.
acceptance_criteria: - FTS-backed retrieval exists for v1\n- Prior review lookup helps refresh flows\n- Later semantic hooks are isolated behind stable interfaces

title: Safety and validation
description: Prove the product stays review-safe, approval-gated, and resumable through explicit validation rather than assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: - Review-safe defaults are enforced\n- End-to-end workflows are testable\n- Refresh identity behavior and OpenCode fallback are validated

title: Run architecture risk spikes and capture ADRs
description: Reduce the highest-risk unknowns before implementation spreads: the OpenCode boundary, browser launch bridge, and artifact storage split. Record any package-shaping outcomes in ADRs.
acceptance_criteria: - Spike outcomes are documented\n- Any package-shaping decisions are captured in ADRs\n- The browser bridge, OpenCode coupling, and artifact storage assumptions are no longer implicit

title: Define core domain schema and finding fingerprint model
description: Specify the first-class entities for sessions, runs, findings, finding fingerprints, artifacts, outbound drafts, posted actions, and config layers. Include the refresh and finding identity rules needed to avoid duplicate explosions.
acceptance_criteria: - Core entities and invariants are defined\n- Triage state and outbound state are distinct\n- Finding fingerprint and refresh classification rules are explicit

title: Implement local storage, migrations, and artifact budget classes
description: Create the SQLite-plus-FTS storage layer and define the storage split between primary rows and larger content-addressed artifacts so the TUI and refresh workflows remain responsive.
acceptance_criteria: - Migration flow exists\n- Storage schema supports core Roger entities\n- Artifact budget classes prevent large payloads from bloating primary tables

title: Define the Roger-to-OpenCode session linkage
description: Decide the exact Roger session contract with OpenCode: how sessions are linked, reopened, summarized after compaction, and kept resumable outside Roger-specific UI.
acceptance_criteria: - Session linkage contract is documented\n- Resume and fallback behavior are explicit\n- Integration avoids unnecessary dependence on fragile internals if a stable boundary exists

title: Encode staged review prompts and structured findings
description: Implement the review engine stages for exploration and deep review, and persist outputs as structured findings with evidence, lineage, and stage provenance.
acceptance_criteria: - Prompt stages are executable independently\n- Findings are persisted as structured objects\n- Evidence links and stage provenance are retained

title: Implement refresh behavior and finding lifecycle transitions
description: Support reruns after new commits by classifying findings as carried forward, superseded, resolved, or stale instead of duplicating them blindly.
acceptance_criteria: - Refresh can map new findings to prior findings\n- Lifecycle transitions cover carried-forward, superseded, resolved, and stale states\n- Approval lineage stays intact across refreshes

title: Implement session-aware CLI review, resume, findings, status, and refresh
description: Create the CLI layer that can infer repo context where possible and drive the usable Roger review loop without relying on the extension.
acceptance_criteria: - Review and resume commands work from the shell\n- Findings, status, and refresh are accessible\n- Repo-context inference works where a PR target can be resolved safely

title: Build the TUI findings workflow and local approval surfaces
description: Implement the main Roger TUI shell, findings list/detail flow, evidence navigation, and local approval surface for outbound GitHub drafts.
acceptance_criteria: - Users can enter an active review session\n- Findings can be triaged quickly from the TUI\n- Outbound drafts can be reviewed and approved locally before posting

title: Model outbound drafts and posted-action audit trail
description: Define the local representation for review comments, questions, and suggestions awaiting approval, and preserve the exact payload that was eventually posted.
acceptance_criteria: - Outbound drafts are linked to findings\n- Approval is explicit before posting\n- Posted payload, outcome, and remote identifiers are snapshotted locally

title: Implement GitHub adapter and validate the browser launch bridge
description: Resolve PR metadata locally, prepare outbound actions through an adapter boundary, and validate a daemonless browser-to-local launch or resume flow with a clear fallback.
acceptance_criteria: - PR targets can be resolved and adapted locally\n- Browser launch/resume path works without a persistent daemon\n- Fallback launch path exists when the bridge is unavailable

title: Implement extension UI with bounded v1 status behavior
description: Inject Roger actions into GitHub PR pages, but keep the v1 extension honest: launch and resume matter most, and status can degrade gracefully if live sync would require hidden infrastructure.
acceptance_criteria: - Launch/resume actions are available from GitHub PR pages\n- Status behavior is explicit and bounded for v1\n- Extension remains a thin surface over the Roger core

title: Implement worktree preparation and conservative named-instance sharing
description: Support isolated review environments and multiple named Roger instances, starting with conservative snapshot or copy semantics rather than ambitious fine-grained sync.
acceptance_criteria: - Review worktrees can be prepared and tracked\n- Multiple named instances can coexist safely\n- State-sharing semantics are conservative and explicit

title: Add FTS-backed prior-review lookup and memory hooks
description: Provide fast retrieval of prior findings and review artifacts for refresh flows and later skill or semantic-search extensions, without making semantic search a v1 blocker.
acceptance_criteria: - Prior review lookup is fast and locally queryable\n- FTS-backed retrieval supports refresh and triage\n- Semantic search remains an optional later layer behind stable interfaces

title: Validate end-to-end review safety, fallback, and refresh identity
description: Run the validation matrix across CLI launch, TUI triage, GitHub launch, posting approval, OpenCode fallback, and refresh identity behavior so the planning stage exits with real proof rather than optimism.
acceptance_criteria: - CLI, TUI, and GitHub launch paths have repeatable validation steps\n- Nothing posts without explicit approval\n- OpenCode fallback and refresh identity behavior are tested explicitly
